reply_data.text: @gudanglifehack the social media manager use case is legit. we built https://t.co/UAhE1GeZ0m around exactly this - Claude finds relevant Reddit/X threads and drafts replies that fit the actual conversation. tricky part is keeping the voice consistent and not sounding like a bot. takes some

@gudanglifehack Prompts are the easy part. Execution is where 99% fail.

After helping 100k+ users grow, the pattern:

→ Get perfect plan
→ Post 2 weeks
→ No results
→ Quit

Missing piece? Initial momentum.

Zero engagement = algorithm death. Social proof first, then AI compounds on top.

@gudanglifehack AI prompts are a great starting point, but here's what most people miss:

The prompts give you a framework. But the real edge is using them WITH your own data.

What I've seen work best after managing social growth for 100k+ users:

1. Feed the AI your top 10 performing posts →
